name: Grilled Cardamom Nan Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, dissolve yeast in the water and stir in the honey. Let stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. Add the 1 tablespoon olive oil, the flour, salt, and cardamom to the dough. Stir until it forms a cohesive mass.
3. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 6 minutes.
4. Divide the dough into 8 pieces and shape each piece into a ball. Set the balls on an oiled baking sheet and brush with oil. Cover loosely with plastic and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, 1 to 2 hours. (Dough can be refrigerated overnight; punch down balls before proceeding)
5. On a lightly floured surface, roll out each ball of dough to an 8-inch disk. Arrange them on oiled baking sheets and let rest for 20 minutes.
6. Lightly grease the grill or heat the grill pan/cast-iron skillet.
7. Brush each nan bread lightly with olive oil and grill until golden on the bottom and light bubbles form on the top, about 1 minute. Turn and cook until golden all over, about 1 minute. Keep warm while cooking the remaining nan breads.
